name: migration-plan-before-edits
summary: Before non-trivial migrations (framework version, DB schema, API contract), write a plan — risks, phases, rollback.
when_to_use: Framework major bumps, DB schema changes, auth swaps, brand renames across many files.

phases.name: Scope
steps: Identify external consumers (APIs, events, public interfaces) — these drive backcompat decisions.

phases.name: Risks
steps: What can break and at what blast radius?
Any window where system is inconsistent (dual-write during DB migration)?

phases.name: Phases
steps: Break into deployable phases. Each: isolated, reversible.
Identify 'point of no return' — after which rollback becomes expensive.
Typical shape: add new → migrate data → switch traffic → remove old.

phases.name: Rollback
steps: What data loss could rollback introduce?
Window before rollback becomes impossible — state it.

phases.name: Execute
steps: agents/migration-lead runs plan (Opus, writes plan before code).
Phase-by-phase: implement, test, deploy, verify, commit, proceed.
User confirmation at every point of no return.

exit_criteria: Plan exists, rollback tested, phases atomic up to point of no return.
anti_patterns: Skipping plan on 'just a version bump'.
Merging phases to save time — couples failures.
